english: used before a negative for emphasis, usu. as a retort

etymology.description: Originally depicted two people standing side-by-side -> representing alignment or togetherness
etymology.interpretation: The character 並 originally depicted two individuals standing next to each other, emphasizing the concept of being together or aligned, which relates directly to its meaning of combined action or inclusivity.
etymology.simplification: The character 并 simplifies 並 by reducing the repetition of strokes representing two people to a single form, conveying the same meaning through a straightforward design.

examples.chinese: 他并不是不想去参加聚会(他並不是不想去參加聚會)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bìng bù shì bù xiǎng qù cānjiā jùhuì.
examples.english: He doesn't actually not want to attend the party.

examples.chinese: 我并不觉得这个问题很复杂(我並不覺得這個問題很複雜)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bìng bù juéde zhège wèntí hěn fùzá.
examples.english: I don't think this problem is complicated at all.
